statute: Iowa Code § 91C (Contractor Registration); Iowa Code § 572 (Mechanics Liens)
license_notes: Iowa requires registration (not a full license) for contractors performing more than $2,000 in work annually. Specialty trades (plumbing, HVAC, electrical) require state licenses.

mandatory_clauses.label: Contractor registration disclosure
Iowa lien rights notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under Iowa law (Iowa Code § 572), subcontractors, materialmen, and suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property if they are not paid. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Citations from $500 to $5,000 for unregistered contractors. No specific license requirement for filing mechanics liens.
notes: Iowa requires contractor registration for those performing over $2,000 in work per year. Plumbing, HVAC, electrical, and sheet metal require state licenses. Bond requirement: $5,000. Federal 3-day notice satisfies state cancellation requirements.
